actions.name: BROWSER
actions.description: Single BROWSER action — control whichever browser target is registered. Targets are pluggable: `workspace` (electrobun-embedded BrowserView, the default; falls back to a JSDOM web mode when the desktop bridge isn't configured), `bridge` (the user's real Chrome/Safari via the Agent Browser Bridge companion extension), and `computeruse` (a local puppeteer-driven Chromium via plugin-computeruse). The agent uses what is available — the BrowserService picks the active target when none is specified. Use `subaction: "autofill-login"` with `domain` (and optional `username`, `submit`) to vault-gated autofill into an open workspace tab.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Browser action to perform. Legacy subaction is also accepted.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: tabAction
parameters.description: Tab operation when subaction is tab

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Required when subaction is autofill-login: registrable hostname (e.g. `github.com`).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: When using autofill-login: specific saved login; omit for most recently modified.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: When using autofill-login: submit the form after filling (default false).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Session or tab id to target

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL for open or navigate

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector for click, type, or wait

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text for type

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Keyboard key for press

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: Scroll distance in pixels

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Command timeout in milliseconds

parameters.name: script
parameters.description: Script for eval

parameters.name: watchMode
parameters.description: Hint that the user is watching; prefers realistic-* subactions for click/fill so the cursor moves visibly and pointer events fire faithfully.

parameters.name: cursorDurationMs
parameters.description: Cursor animation duration (ms) for realistic-* subactions

parameters.name: perCharDelayMs
parameters.description: Per-character delay for realistic-type/realistic-fill (ms)

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: Replace existing input value when filling (vs append) — applies to realistic-fill

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Cursor target X (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Cursor target Y (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

actions.descriptionCompressed: Browser tab/page control: open/navigate/click/type/screenshot/state; subaction autofill-login + domain autofill vault-gated credential into workspace tab pre-authorized in Settings Vault Logins. Bridge settings/status use MANAGE_BROWSER_BRIDGE.

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER with the provided parameters.

actions.name: COMPUTER_USE
actions.description:
computer_use:
  purpose: Canonical cross-platform computer-use action for real desktop interaction on macOS, Linux, and Windows when direct computer operation is required.
  guidance: Take a screenshot before acting. After each desktop action, the result includes a screenshot when available. Use this standard plugin action, not a LifeOps wrapper, for Finder/Desktop/native-app/browser/file/terminal workflows on the owner's machine.
  actions: screenshot/click/click_with_modifiers/double_click/right_click/mouse_move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Desktop action to perform.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Target [x, y] pixel coordinate.

parameters.name: startCoordinate
parameters.description: Start [x, y] pixel coordinate for drag.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description: Modifier keys to hold during click_with_modifiers, e.g. ['cmd', 'shift'] or ['ctrl'].

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Single key or combo string depending on action.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: clicks
parameters.description: Number of clicks for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: scrollDirection
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: scrollAmount
parameters.description: Scroll tick count.

exampleCalls.user: Use COMPUTER_USE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: DESKTOP
actions.description: Single DESKTOP action — dispatches local desktop operations through the computer-use service.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Desktop operation group. Reserved future values: screenshot, ocr, detect_elements (currently on COMPUTER_USE).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Sub-op verb for the chosen op (e.g. read/write for file, list/focus for window, execute for terminal).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Primary file or directory path (file op).

parameters.name: filepath
parameters.description: Upstream alias for path (file op).

parameters.name: dirpath
parameters.description: Upstream alias for directory path (file op).

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Content for write, append, or upload (file op).

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: Encoding for read/download (file op).

parameters.name: oldText
parameters.description: Replacement source text for edit (file op).

parameters.name: newText
parameters.description: Replacement destination text for edit (file op).

parameters.name: old_text
parameters.description: Upstream edit source text (file op).

parameters.name: new_text
parameters.description: Upstream edit destination text (file op).

parameters.name: find
parameters.description: Upstream alias for old_text (file op).

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: Upstream alias for new_text (file op).

parameters.name: windowId
parameters.description: Window identifier (window op).

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.description: Window title or app-name query (window op).

parameters.name: arrangement
parameters.description: Layout for window arrange: tile, cascade, vertical, or horizontal.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Target X coordinate for window move.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Target Y coordinate for window move.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command (terminal op execute / execute_command).

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Working directory for terminal connect or execute.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Terminal session ID alias.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Upstream terminal session ID alias.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text for terminal type.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in seconds (terminal op).

parameters.name: timeoutSeconds
parameters.description: Alias for timeout (terminal op).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Single DESKTOP action; op=file|window|terminal dispatches to the matching computer-use op (screenshot/ocr/detect_elements reserved).

exampleCalls.user: Use DESKTOP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: FILE
actions.description: Read, write, edit, search, find, or list workspace files through one FILE action. Choose action=read/write/edit/grep/glob/ls. All paths must be absolute unless an operation explicitly defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: File operation to run.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path for read/write/edit operations.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute file or directory path for grep/glob/ls. Defaults to the session cwd where supported.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Full file contents for action=write.

parameters.name: old_string
parameters.description: Exact substring to replace for action=edit.

parameters.name: new_string
parameters.description: Replacement substring for action=edit.

parameters.name: replace_all
parameters.description: For action=edit, replace every occurrence instead of requiring one match.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Regex for action=grep or glob pattern for action=glob.

parameters.name: glob
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep glob filter for action=grep.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep file type for action=grep.

parameters.name: output_mode
parameters.description: For action=grep: content, files_with_matches, or count.

parameters.name: -A
parameters.description: For action=grep content mode, lines after each match.

parameters.name: -B
parameters.description: For action=grep content mode, lines before each match.

parameters.name: -C
parameters.description: For action=grep content mode, lines around each match.

parameters.name: case_insensitive
parameters.description: For action=grep, match case-insensitively.

parameters.name: multiline
parameters.description: For action=grep, enable multiline regex matching.

parameters.name: head_limit
parameters.description: For action=grep, truncate output to the first N lines.

parameters.name: show_line_numbers
parameters.description: For action=grep, include 1-based line numbers in content output.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: For action=read, zero-based line offset.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: For action=read, max number of lines to return.

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: For action=ls, glob patterns to exclude.

exampleCalls.user: Use FILE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GITHUB
actions.description: GitHub umbrella for pull requests, issues, and notification triage. Use action=pr_list/pr_review/issue_create/issue_assign/issue_close/issue_reopen/issue_comment/issue_label/notification_triage.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: GitHub operation to run.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: Repository in owner/name form.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Pull request or issue number.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: PR state for pr_list: open, closed, or all.

parameters.name: author
parameters.description: Optional PR author username filter for pr_list.

parameters.name: review_action
parameters.description: For action=pr_review: approve, request-changes, or comment.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Issue title for action=issue_create.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Issue body, issue comment body, or PR review body.

parameters.name: assignees
parameters.description: GitHub usernames to assign.

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: Labels to apply on issue create or issue_label.

parameters.name: as
parameters.description: Identity to use: agent or user.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Optional GitHub account id from GITHUB_ACCOUNTS. Defaults by role.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true for GitHub write operations.

exampleCalls.user: Use GITHUB with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LINEAR
actions.description: Manage Linear issues, comments, and activity. Operations: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. The op is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Linear: create/get/update/delete issue, create/update/delete/list comment, search issues, get/clear activity.

exampleCalls.user: Use LINEAR with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LIQUIDITY
actions.description: Single LP/liquidity management action. action=onboard|list_pools|open|close|reposition|list_positions|get_position|set_preferences. dex=orca|raydium|meteora|uniswap|aerodrome|pancakeswap selects the protocol; chain=solana|evm is inferred from dex when omitted.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Liquidity operation: onboard, list_pools, open, close, reposition, list_positions, get_position, set_preferences.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: Chain for the LP operation.

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: DEX/protocol name.

parameters.name: pool
parameters.description: Pool id/address for open, close, reposition, or position lookup.

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: LP position id/mint/address.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Liquidity amount for open, close, or reposition operations.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Desired concentrated liquidity price range.

parameters.name: tokenA
parameters.description: First token filter or deposit token.

parameters.name: tokenB
parameters.description: Second token filter or deposit token.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Optional numeric EVM chain id.

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Maximum allowed slippage in basis points.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Manage LP positions by action, chain, dex, pool, position, amount, range, token filters.

exampleCalls.user: Use LIQUIDITY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MCP
actions.description: Single MCP entry point. Use action=call_tool to invoke an MCP tool, action=read_resource to read an MCP resource. Cloud runtimes also accept action=search_actions and action=list_connections.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: MCP operation: call_tool | read_resource | search_actions | list_connections

parameters.name: serverName
parameters.description: Optional MCP server name that owns the tool or resource.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: For action=call_tool: optional exact MCP tool name to call.

parameters.name: arguments
parameters.description: For action=call_tool: optional JSON arguments to pass to the selected MCP tool.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: For action=read_resource: exact MCP resource URI to read.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Natural-language description of the tool call or resource to select; for action=search_actions, the keyword query.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: For action=search_actions: filter results to a single connected platform.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: For action=search_actions: maximum results to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: For action=search_actions: skip first N results for pagination.

exampleCalls.user: Use MCP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MUSIC
actions.description: Unified music action. Use flat action for everything: library (playlist, play_query, search_youtube, download), playback transport (pause, resume, skip, stop, queue), play_audio, routing, zones.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Flat operation: playlist | play_query | search_youtube | download | pause | resume | skip | stop | queue | play_audio | routing | zones (hyphens and legacy aliases accepted).

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Playlist subaction when op=playlist (save, load, delete, add, …).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search/play/queue query depending on op.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Direct media URL when using play_audio.

parameters.name: playlistName
parameters.description: Playlist name for playlist ops.

parameters.name: song
parameters.description: Song query for playlist add.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Search result limit (YouTube / library helpers).

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true when the underlying operation requires confirmation.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Structured routing operation when using routing (set_mode, start_route, …).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Routing mode for routing operations.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Stream/source id for routing.

parameters.name: targetIds
parameters.description: Routing target ids.

exampleCalls.user: Use MUSIC with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MUSIC_GENERATION
actions.description: Generate music through Suno. Use action generate for a simple prompt, custom for style/BPM/key/reference parameters, or extend for an existing audio_id and duration.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Suno operation: generate, custom, or extend.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Music prompt for generate/custom.

parameters.name: audio_id
parameters.description: Existing Suno audio id for extend.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Generation duration or extension seconds.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Suno music generation router action: generate, custom, extend.

exampleCalls.user: Use MUSIC_GENERATION with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MYSTICISM_PAYMENT
actions.description: Payment router for the active mysticism reading session. Set action to 'check' to read payment status, or 'request' to ask the user to pay (set amount or include $X.XX in the message).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation: check or request.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: For request — payment amount as a string (e.g. '3.00').

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: For check — optional entity id whose active reading payment should be checked. Defaults to the current sender.

parameters.name: roomId
parameters.description: For check — optional room id whose active reading payment should be checked. Defaults to the current room.

exampleCalls.user: Use MYSTICISM_PAYMENT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: OWNER_FINANCES
actions.description: Owner finances: payment sources, transaction imports, spending summaries, recurring charges, and subscription audits.

actions.name: OWNER_HEALTH
actions.description: Owner health telemetry reads across HealthKit, Google Fit, Strava, Fitbit, Withings, or Oura. Actions: today, trend, by_metric, status.

actions.name: OWNER_SCREENTIME
actions.description: Owner screen-time and activity analytics across local activity, app usage, and browser reports.

actions.name: PERSONAL_ASSISTANT
actions.description: Owner personal-assistant workflows. Use action=book_travel for real travel booking, action=scheduling for scheduling negotiation, and action=sign_document for document-signature flows that must be queued for owner approval.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Assistant workflow to run.

exampleCalls.user: Use PERSONAL_ASSISTANT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: PLAY_EMOTE
actions.description: Play a one-shot emote animation on your 3D VRM avatar, then return to idle.

parameters.name: emote
parameters.description: Required emote ID to play once silently before returning to idle.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Play one-shot VRM avatar emote animation. Silent visual side-action.

exampleCalls.user: Use PLAY_EMOTE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: READING
actions.description: Mystical reading router. Set type to tarot, astrology, or iching, and action to start (begin a new reading), followup (reveal the next element), or deepen (more interpretation for the most-recent element).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Reading type: tarot, astrology, or iching.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: start, followup, or deepen.

parameters.name: question
parameters.description: Optional question or focus for the reading.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Optional additional context (e.g., birth data hint for astrology).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Mystical readings: tarot, astrology, iching; actions: start, followup, deepen.

exampleCalls.user: Use READING with the provided parameters.

actions.name: RS_2004
actions.description: Drive the 2004scape game agent. Choose one action (walk_to, chop, mine, fish, burn, cook, fletch, craft, smith, drop, pickup, equip, unequip, use, use_on_item, use_on_object, open, close, deposit, withdraw, buy, sell, attack, cast_spell, set_style, eat, talk, navigate_dialog, interact_object, open_door, pickpocket). For open/close, set target='bank' or target='shop' (or include npc to imply shop). Per-action fields go in params.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to run.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional JSON object containing the fields required by the chosen op.

actions.descriptionCompressed: rs_2004 actions (walk_to, skills, inventory, bank, shop, combat, interact)

exampleCalls.user: Use RS_2004 with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SCAPE
actions.description: Drive the 'scape (xRSPS) game agent. Pick one action: walk_to (x,z,run?), attack (npcId), chat_public (message), eat (item?), drop (item), set_goal (title,notes?), complete_goal (status?,goalId?,notes?), remember (notes,kind?,weight?). Returns success and a short status message; the autonomous loop already handles its own dispatch — this is the planner-facing surface.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to run.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional JSON object containing the fields required by the chosen action.

exampleCalls.user: Use SCAPE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SHELL
actions.description: Execute a shell command via the configured local shell. Runs synchronously in the session cwd by default. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code. Hard timeout kills the command. Paths under the configured blocklist are off-limits as cwd.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command to run; executed via /bin/bash -c <command>.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Five to ten word humanly-readable summary of the command.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Hard timeout in ms; clamped to [100, 600000]. Default 120000.

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Absolute working directory; must not resolve under a blocked path. Defaults to the session cwd.

exampleCalls.user: Use SHELL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SHOPIFY
actions.description: Manage a Shopify store. Actions: search (read-only catalog browsing across products, orders, and customers), products (CRUD on products), inventory (stock adjustments), orders (list/update orders), customers (CRUD on customers). Action is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: search, products, inventory, orders, customers. Inferred from message text when omitted.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search term for action=search.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Search scope for action=search: all, products, orders, or customers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results per searched Shopify category.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Shopify: search, products, inventory, orders, customers.

exampleCalls.user: Use SHOPIFY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SKILL
actions.description: Manage skill catalog. Operations: search (browse available skills), details (info about a specific skill), sync (refresh catalog from registry), toggle (enable/disable installed skill), install (install from registry), uninstall (remove non-bundled skill). For invoking an enabled skill, use USE_SKILL instead.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Skill catalog: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall.

exampleCalls.user: Use SKILL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TODO
actions.description: Manage the user's todo list. Actions: write (replace the list with `todos:[{id?, content, status, activeForm?}]`), create (add one), update (change by id), complete, cancel, delete, list, clear. Todos are user-scoped (entityId), persistent, and shared across rooms for the same user.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: write, create, update, complete, cancel, delete, list, clear.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Todo id (update/complete/cancel/delete).

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Imperative form, e.g. 'Add tests' (create/update).

parameters.name: activeForm
parameters.description: Present-continuous form, e.g. 'Adding tests' (create/update).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: pending | in_progress | completed | cancelled.

parameters.name: parentTodoId
parameters.description: Parent todo id for sub-tasks (create/update).

parameters.name: todos
parameters.description: Array of {id?, content, status, activeForm?} for action=write. Replaces the user's list for this conversation.

parameters.name: includeCompleted
parameters.description: Include completed/cancelled todos in action=list output.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return for action=list.

exampleCalls.user: Use TODO with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TUNNEL
actions.description: Tunnel operations dispatched by `action`: start, stop, status. The `start` action accepts an optional `port` (defaults to 3000); `stop` and `status` take no parameters. Backed by whichever tunnel plugin is active (local Tailscale CLI, Eliza Cloud headscale, or ngrok).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Which tunnel sub-operation to run. One of: start, stop, status.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: Parameters forwarded to the selected sub-op. For `start`, optionally `{ port: number }`. `stop` and `status` take no parameters.

exampleCalls.user: Use TUNNEL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: USE_SKILL
actions.description: Invoke an enabled skill by slug. The skill's instructions or script run and the result returns to the conversation.
actions.descriptionCompressed: Invoke an enabled skill by slug.

actions.name: VOICE_CALL
actions.description: Place a phone call to a given number using the Android Telecom service.

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: Phone number to call. Accepts E.164 (`+15551234567`) or local

actions.descriptionCompressed: Place a phone call via Android Telecom. Requires CALL_PHONE permission.

exampleCalls.user: Use VOICE_CALL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WORKTREE
actions.description: Manage the current git worktree stack. Choose action=enter to create and switch into an isolated worktree, or action=exit to leave the current worktree and optionally remove it.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Worktree operation to run.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: For action=enter, optional worktree branch/dir name. Defaults to auto-*.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: For action=enter, optional absolute worktree directory within sandbox roots.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: For action=enter, optional base ref. Defaults to HEAD.

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.description: For action=exit, remove the popped worktree directory with git worktree remove --force.

exampleCalls.user: Use WORKTREE with the provided parameters.
